pages.rawText:
Partial Hospitalization Program for Females | Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment 
 
 

 

 

 
 

 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 
 Skip to Content 
 

 

 
 
 
 

 

 

 
 
 Menu 
 

 
 (630) 749-2493 
 
 

 

 
 
 Menu 
 

 
 
 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 
 
 Successful Treatment Results 

 Testimonials 
 
 

 

 

 
 
 
 

To request medical records: CALL (615) 861-6000 x 5 or

 Email Your Request 

 
 
 
 

 
 Partial Hospitalization Program for Women 

 There are many ways to heal from  eating disorders and other behavioral health concerns .  In many cases, t he  partial hospitalization program (PHP) environment  can be  an ideal space for women  to  successfully  progress  in their recovery.     

 To help meet  you where you are in your recovery journey, Timberline Knolls  offers a partial hospitalization program for women age 18 and older .  Our PHP  empowers women  to  live the lives they deserve.   

 Benefits of Partial Hospitalization Programming   

 If you are s truggling with  an eating disorder,  a  mental health concern,  the effects of trauma,  or  a  substance use disorder   and  you don’t seek professional help or programming , it   can have a significant impact on your ability to function .  The partial hospitalization program at Timberline Knolls  can help you address the concerns that have disrupted your daily life.   

 There are countless benefits to  choosing the PHP at Timberline Knolls , including:   

 

 Structured  e nvironment:  When you  take part in our PHP, you will follow a structured schedule each day  that ensures  that  you receive  a high level of  care.   

 Flexib le  l iving  a rrangements:   After each treatment day is over,  you can go home or stay in one of our on-campus lodges.   

 Real-life skill – building :  You’ll have the opportunity to practice the skills and coping techniques you learn  in  our PHP  in real-life environments and bring any learning back to the program.   

 

 Depending on your current needs, y ou  may step   down to our PHP from a higher level of care, or you may enter directly  at  the PHP  level. Our expert  staff can help you  determine   if  our partial hospitalization program is the best fit for you or if you  woul d benefit from a different level of care.   

 Supportive Living Environment   

At the Timberline Knolls PHP, we offer supportive living housing options to our clients at an affordable cost. The supported housing environment advances our clients’ recovery journeys and compliments our Continuing Care Outpatient Program by offering individuals the structure and support of recovery- focused living. With our supportive housing, our clients have: access to staff support; comfortable housing accommodations; transportation to and from clinical programming, as well as 24/7 peer support.

 The  PHP  Experience   

 We recognize that every woman who walks through our doors has  a  completely different  life experience, medical histor y , and genetic background that  can  impact the way  a behavioral health concern  may affect them .  That’s why w e individualize   care  for every woman who  takes part in the partial hospitalization program at Timberline Knolls.    

 With those influences in mind, you’ll take part in our partial hospitalization program five or seven days a week for up to three weeks depending on your specific needs and progress.    

 Elements of your care may include:   

 

 Group, family, and individual therapies   

 Experiential therap ies   

 Basic medical care   

 Medication management   

 

 Topics addressed in group therapy sessions may include  body image, medication education, the 12 Steps, and  skill – building.   Most women  also  take part in two individual therapy sessions and one family session a week , but  we can schedule  these sessions  based on how you’re progressing  in your recovery .   

 Evidence-Based Treatment Modalities 

 

Dialectical behavior therapy (DBT)

Principles of recovery

Acceptance & commitment therapy (ACT)

Expressive therapy

Motivational interviewing

Solution-focused therapy

Trauma-informed care

Psychoeducation

 

 Continuing Care   

 Making the decision to  seek support for  an  eating disorder , a mood disorder, an addiction, or the effects of trauma  is a life-changing step. But the days, weeks, and months after  you complete the partial hospitalization program at Timberline Knolls are just as important as your time in our care.    

 We  start   creating  your  discharge plan  within 48 hours after you begin taking part in our PHP  so  that  we can  provide you  with the resources you need to continue  your progress. Your continuing care plan will include recommendations for  any necessary  follow-up care,   community resources, support groups, and information on  the Timberline Knolls  alumnae network.   

 With  the expert care of the staff at Timberline Knolls , you can start living a healthier, more fulfilling life.   

 This content was reviewed and approved by the clinical staff at Timberline Knolls .   

 

To view this embedded video, please edit your cookie preferences and allow Personalization cookies.

 Cookie Preferences 

 Our Programs 

 About Us 

 

 
 We help each woman and adolescent girl rediscover and harness her strengths in five core aspects of the self: 

 
 
 

 

 

 “ Hi, My name is Angel. I was admitted to residential treatment at Timberline Knolls in 2016 to treat my depression. At that time I was unemployed, home alone, and labeled med resistant. Though I did not socialize much, I benefited because I was no longer alone.

I elected to go to the partial hospitalization program at TK thereafter. This was because I wanted to continue with the DBT principles, continue with yoga, and continue speaking with the chaplain. Choosing to stay with TK resulted in me regaining my voice. In PHP I started engaging socially with the TK community. I also started to unpack my relationship with my father, with my father and our family therapist; a process that I prepared for in residential. Residential treatment at Timberline Knolls and continuing with partial hospitalization ensured that I could keep building on my recovery, namely in my relationships with spirituality, with new people, and with myself.

- Angel T

 

 ” 

 

 
 Why Choose Timberline Knolls? 

 

To view this embedded video, please edit your cookie preferences and allow Personalization cookies.

 Cookie Preferences 

 Individualized Care from Exemplary Medical and Clinical Staff 

 Experiential & Spiritual Reinvigoration and Healing for the Whole Family 

 Lifelong Friendships with a Positive Peer Group 

 Secure, Tranquil Setting in a Convenient, Central Location 

 Award Winning On-Campus School 

 
 

 

Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment Center is respected nationwide for its treatment of eating disorders, mental health, and addiction concerns.

 

 

 © 2026 Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment Center / 40 Timberline Drive, Lemont, IL 60439 / (630) 749-2493 

 

If you are unable to read or view this page please call Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment Center at (630) 749-2493 .

 

 

 
 Accessibility Notice 

 Privacy Practices 

 Online Privacy Policy 

 Compliance & Code of Conduct 

 Cookie Preferences 
 

 

 
 

 
 Call 24/7 
 Menu 
 

 
 (630) 749-2493 
 
 

 

 
 
 Cookie Preferences 
 

 
 
 

 
 

 
 
 Top 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Timberline Knolls is Closed

Timberline Knolls is honored to have served the community for nearly 20 years. We have now closed our campuses and are no longer accepting new patients. For additional resources, please visit  https://www.samhsa.gov/ .

        <script>
            /////////////////////////////////////////////
            /// [S T A R T]  O S A N O
            /////////////////////////////////////////////
            let initialCount = 0;
            let iFramesLoaded = false;
            let chatLoaded = false;
            // Count how many of the three consents are true
            function countConsents() {
                let c = Osano.cm;
                let count = 0;
                if (c.analytics) count++;
                if (c.marketing) count++;
                if (c.personalization) count++;
                return count;
            }

            //Wait for document to render or chat/iframes fire too early on implied consent
            document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function () {
                // Run once Osano finishes initializing
                Osano.cm.addEventListener('osano-cm-initialized', function() {
                    initialCount = countConsents();
                    if (Osano.cm.personalization) {
                        loadIframeEmbeds();
                        loadNiceChat();
                    }
                });

                Osano.cm.addEventListener('osano-cm-consent-saved', function() {
                    const newCount = countConsents();
                    if (Osano.cm.personalization) {
                        loadIframeEmbeds();
                        loadNiceChat();
                    }
                    // If any consent is removed, refresh page
                    if (newCount < initialCount) {
                        location.reload();
                    } else {initialCount = newCount;}
                });

                const showCookiePreferences = document.getElementsByClassName('show-cookie-preferences');
                const link = document.getElementById('osano_drawer');
                if (!link) return;
                // Ensure CMP is ready
                Osano.cm.addEventListener('osano-cm-initialized', function () {
                    link.addEventListener('click', function (e) {
                        e.preventDefault();
                        if (Osano.cm.drawerOpen) {
                            Osano.cm.hideDrawer();
                        } else {
                            // Most installs: no args; some docs show a token—fallback if needed.
                            try { Osano.cm.showDrawer(); }
                            catch { Osano.cm.showDrawer('osano-cm-dom-info-dialog-open'); }
                        }
                    });
                });
                // Hook to drawer
                for (i=0;i<showCookiePreferences.length;i++) {
                    showCookiePreferences.item(i).addEventListener('click', function () {
                        Osano.cm.showDrawer();
                    });
                }
            });
            /////////////////////////////////////////////
            /// [E N D]  O S A N O
            /////////////////////////////////////////////
            function loadNiceChat() {
                            }
            function loadIframeEmbeds() {
                if (!iFramesLoaded) {
                    const embeddedIframes = document.querySelectorAll('.acadia_iframe_inner, .acadia_video_inner');
                    for (i=0;i<embeddedIframes.length;i++) {
                        const embeddedIframe = embeddedIframes.item(i);
                        embeddedIframe.innerHTML = '';
                        const iframe = document.createElement('iframe');
                        iframe.setAttribute('src', embeddedIframe.getAttribute('data-src'));
                        iframe.setAttribute('class', embeddedIframe.getAttribute('data-class'));
                        iframe.setAttribute('data-cookie-consent', 'yes');
                        iframe.setAttribute('allowfullscreen', '');
                        iframe.setAttribute('loading', 'lazy');
                        embeddedIframe.appendChild(iframe);
                    }
                    iFramesLoaded = true;
                }
            }
        </script>

class="vc_row-full-width vc_clearfix sp"></div></div></div></div><div id="main" class="sp30"><div class="container clearfix vc_row"><div id="content" class="vc_column vc_col-xs-12 vc_col-md-8 sp30"><div id="article"><article id="post-4802" class="article acadia_page_cats-program"><div class="entry-content"><div class="wpb-content-wrapper"><div class="vc_row vc_row_15 vc_row-fluid sp"><div class="vc_column vc_column_16 vc_column_container vc_col-sm-12 sp"><div class="vc_column-inner "><div class="wpb_wrapper"><div class="acadia_text acadia_text_1 sp"><p>By recognizing that you or your loved one may be struggling with a serious problem, you’ve already taken an important step toward a better future. We’re here to guide you through the next phase of getting help.</p>
<h2>The Importance of Education in Residential Treatment</h2>
<p>When it comes to recovery from eating disorders, addiction, and other co-occurring disorders, we believe the combination of individualized and group therapy, a therapeutic residential treatment environment, and supportive education are critical.</p>
<p>Our goal is not only to help adolescent girls and women recover from their emotional difficulties but also to help them cope and thrive in the world around them.</p>
<p>That’s why our educational program provides a dynamic, therapeutic, structured community where students can learn, achieve and be responsible while receiving the nurturing and sustenance they need to enhance their academic lives.</p>
<p>By focusing on a program of education, treatment, and supervision, our team has had tremendous success. We believe in dynamic collaboration. By giving our students the right attention and expertise, they can achieve their highest emotional, social and academic potential.</p>
<p>Every student is different. That’s why we develop a tailored educational plan for each individual. This plan considers the resident’s current level of achievement, ability to learn and the requirements of her home school district. Instruction in core subjects, elective subjects, advanced placement courses, ACT/SAT prep, GED prep, and online courses is provided where appropriate.</p>
<h2>Our Program Includes:</h2>
<ul>
<li>Certified teachers who are specialists in their field of study and instruct in the four core areas of study, English, math, science, and social studies. They are experienced in working with students of all learning levels from middle school through high school, and with all emotional challenges such as school anxiety, school refusal, disorganization, and perfectionism.</li>
<li>Our teachers collaborate with home school districts across the country, parents/guardians, and the Timberline Knolls therapeutic team to provide outstanding individualized educational services while students work on their recovery.</li>
<li>Our students are in attendance at the TK Academy every weekday which follows a traditional school calendar. This helps normalize their day and prepares them for re-entry to their home school.</li>
<li>Timberline Knolls Academy offers a low student/teacher ratio.</li>
<li>Our students have highly monitored Internet access during school hours.</li>
<li>We offer an extended school year program for students who require extra time to complete their coursework.</li>
</ul>
<h2>Philosophy</h2>
<p>TK Academy seeks to establish a warm and supportive classroom environment. The students are encouraged to take positive risks, evaluate their own work and behavior, and monitor their choices internally thereby developing an improved sense of self.</p>
<h2>School of Excellence</h2>
<p>Additionally, via the award-winning TK Academy, we give students access to an environment for integrative learning opportunities. This allows residents to not only continue their academic progress, but excel, in a setting fully integrated with their clinical treatment. Providing up to two hours a day of educational instruction, the school’s certified teachers, certified teachers’ aides, and academic coordinator partner with students, their treatment teams, and their home school districts to create individualized education plans aligned with Illinois state standards, national standards, grade-level promotion and the exit criteria required for graduation.</p>
<h2>What You Need to Know</h2>
<p>Students remain enrolled in their own home school district when they come to Timberline Knolls.  This allows our academy staff to collaborate closely with each home school district on the specific work each student is involved in; credit is issued by the home school district and appears on the home school district transcript. In addition, students are encouraged to bring a week’s worth of school work with them upon admission to Timberline Knolls.</p>
<h2>More Information</h2>

TK Academy Offers Education Support & Learning for Adolescents & Teens | Timberline Knolls

 Testimonials 
 
 

 

 

 
 
 
 

To request medical records: CALL (615) 861-6000 x 5 or

 Email Your Request 

 
 
 
 

 
 Educational Support for Teens & Young Girls 

By recognizing that you or your loved one may be struggling with a serious problem, you’ve already taken an important step toward a better future. We’re here to guide you through the next phase of getting help.

The Importance of Education in Residential Treatment

When it comes to recovery from eating disorders, addiction, and other co-occurring disorders, we believe the combination of individualized and group therapy, a therapeutic residential treatment environment, and supportive education are critical.

Our goal is not only to help adolescent girls and women recover from their emotional difficulties but also to help them cope and thrive in the world around them.

That’s why our educational program provides a dynamic, therapeutic, structured community where students can learn, achieve and be responsible while receiving the nurturing and sustenance they need to enhance their academic lives.

By focusing on a program of education, treatment, and supervision, our team has had tremendous success. We believe in dynamic collaboration. By giving our students the right attention and expertise, they can achieve their highest emotional, social and academic potential.

Every student is different. That’s why we develop a tailored educational plan for each individual. This plan considers the resident’s current level of achievement, ability to learn and the requirements of her home school district. Instruction in core subjects, elective subjects, advanced placement courses, ACT/SAT prep, GED prep, and online courses is provided where appropriate.

Our Program Includes:

 

Certified teachers who are specialists in their field of study and instruct in the four core areas of study, English, math, science, and social studies. They are experienced in working with students of all learning levels from middle school through high school, and with all emotional challenges such as school anxiety, school refusal, disorganization, and perfectionism.

Our teachers collaborate with home school districts across the country, parents/guardians, and the Timberline Knolls therapeutic team to provide outstanding individualized educational services while students work on their recovery.

Our students are in attendance at the TK Academy every weekday which follows a traditional school calendar. This helps normalize their day and prepares them for re-entry to their home school.

Timberline Knolls Academy offers a low student/teacher ratio.

Our students have highly monitored Internet access during school hours.

We offer an extended school year program for students who require extra time to complete their coursework.

 

Philosophy

TK Academy seeks to establish a warm and supportive classroom environment. The students are encouraged to take positive risks, evaluate their own work and behavior, and monitor their choices internally thereby developing an improved sense of self.

School of Excellence

Additionally, via the award-winning TK Academy, we give students access to an environment for integrative learning opportunities. This allows residents to not only continue their academic progress, but excel, in a setting fully integrated with their clinical treatment. Providing up to two hours a day of educational instruction, the school’s certified teachers, certified teachers’ aides, and academic coordinator partner with students, their treatment teams, and their home school districts to create individualized education plans aligned with Illinois state standards, national standards, grade-level promotion and the exit criteria required for graduation.

What You Need to Know

Students remain enrolled in their own home school district when they come to Timberline Knolls.  This allows our academy staff to collaborate closely with each home school district on the specific work each student is involved in; credit is issued by the home school district and appears on the home school district transcript. In addition, students are encouraged to bring a week’s worth of school work with them upon admission to Timberline Knolls.

More Information

 

To view this embedded video, please edit your cookie preferences and allow Personalization cookies.

 Cookie Preferences 

 Our Programs 

 Start the Admissions Process 

 

 
 We help each woman and adolescent girl rediscover and harness her strengths in five core aspects of the self: 

 
 
 

 

 

 “ My time at Timberline Knolls was extremely effective for my overall health; physical, mental, emotional and spiritual. ” 

– A former resident

 

 
 Why Choose Timberline Knolls? 

 

To view this embedded video, please edit your cookie preferences and allow Personalization cookies.

 Cookie Preferences 

 Individualized Care from Exemplary Medical and Clinical Staff 

 Experiential & Spiritual Reinvigoration and Healing for the Whole Family 

 Lifelong Friendships with a Positive Peer Group 

 Secure, Tranquil Setting in a Convenient, Central Location 

 Award Winning On-Campus School 

 
 

 

Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment Center is respected nationwide for its treatment of eating disorders, mental health, and addiction concerns.

 

 

 © 2026 Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment Center / 40 Timberline Drive, Lemont, IL 60439 / (630) 749-2493 

 

If you are unable to read or view this page please call Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment Center at (630) 749-2493 .

 

 

 
 Accessibility Notice 

 Privacy Practices 

 Online Privacy Policy 

 Compliance & Code of Conduct 

 Cookie Preferences 
 

 

 
 

 
 Call 24/7 
 Menu 
 

 
 (630) 749-2493 
 
 

 

 
 
 Cookie Preferences 
 

 
 
 

 
 

 
 
 Top 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Timberline Knolls is Closed

Timberline Knolls is honored to have served the community for nearly 20 years. We have now closed our campuses and are no longer accepting new patients. For additional resources, please visit  https://www.samhsa.gov/ .

        <script>
            /////////////////////////////////////////////
            /// [S T A R T]  O S A N O

            let initialCount = 0;
            let iFramesLoaded = false;
            let chatLoaded = false;
            // Count how many of the three consents are true
            function countConsents() {
                let c = Osano.cm;
                let count = 0;
                if (c.analytics) count++;
                if (c.marketing) count++;
                if (c.personalization) count++;
                return count;
            }

            //Wait for document to render or chat/iframes fire too early on implied consent
            document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function () {
                // Run once Osano finishes initializing
                Osano.cm.addEventListener('osano-cm-initialized', function() {
                    initialCount = countConsents();
                    if (Osano.cm.personalization) {
                        loadIframeEmbeds();
                        loadNiceChat();
                    }
                });

                Osano.cm.addEventListener('osano-cm-consent-saved', function() {
                    const newCount = countConsents();
                    if (Osano.cm.personalization) {
                        loadIframeEmbeds();
                        loadNiceChat();
                    }
                    // If any consent is removed, refresh page
                    if (newCount < initialCount) {
                        location.reload();
                    } else {initialCount = newCount;}
                });

                const showCookiePreferences = document.getElementsByClassName('show-cookie-preferences');
                const link = document.getElementById('osano_drawer');
                if (!link) return;
                // Ensure CMP is ready
                Osano.cm.addEventListener('osano-cm-initialized', function () {
                    link.addEventListener('click', function (e) {
                        e.preventDefault();
                        if (Osano.cm.drawerOpen) {
                            Osano.cm.hideDrawer();
                        } else {
                            // Most installs: no args; some docs show a token—fallback if needed.
                            try { Osano.cm.showDrawer(); }
                            catch { Osano.cm.showDrawer('osano-cm-dom-info-dialog-open'); }
                        }
                    });
                });
                // Hook to drawer
                for (i=0;i<showCookiePreferences.length;i++) {
                    showCookiePreferences.item(i).addEventListener('click', function () {
                        Osano.cm.showDrawer();
                    });
                }
            });
            /////////////////////////////////////////////
            /// [E N D]  O S A N O
            /////////////////////////////////////////////
            function loadNiceChat() {
                            }
            function loadIframeEmbeds() {
                if (!iFramesLoaded) {
                    const embeddedIframes = document.querySelectorAll('.acadia_iframe_inner, .acadia_video_inner');
                    for (i=0;i<embeddedIframes.length;i++) {
                        const embeddedIframe = embeddedIframes.item(i);
                        embeddedIframe.innerHTML = '';
                        const iframe = document.createElement('iframe');
                        iframe.setAttribute('src', embeddedIframe.getAttribute('data-src'));
                        iframe.setAttribute('class', embeddedIframe.getAttribute('data-class'));
                        iframe.setAttribute('data-cookie-consent', 'yes');
                        iframe.setAttribute('allowfullscreen', '');
                        iframe.setAttribute('loading', 'lazy');
                        embeddedIframe.appendChild(iframe);
                    }
                    iFramesLoaded = true;
                }
            }

class="acadia_headline acadia_headline_1 hero-h1 sp"><span class="headline"><span class="text">Treatment Programs for Women & Girls</span></span></h1></div></div></div></div><div class="vc_row-full-width vc_clearfix sp"></div></div></div></div><div id="main" class="sp30"><div class="container clearfix vc_row"><div id="content" class="vc_column vc_col-xs-12 vc_col-md-8 sp30"><div id="article"><article id="post-709" class="article acadia_page_cats-program"><div class="entry-content"><div class="wpb-content-wrapper"><div class="vc_row vc_row_15 vc_row-fluid sp"><div class="vc_column vc_column_16 vc_column_container vc_col-sm-12 sp"><div class="vc_column-inner "><div class="wpb_wrapper"><div class="acadia_text acadia_text_1 sp"><p><span data-contrast="auto">Timberline Knolls offers </span><span data-contrast="auto">individualized and </span><span data-contrast="auto">age-appropriate services for girls and women whose lives have been impacted by <a href="/eating-disorder/">eating disorders</a>, </span><span data-contrast="auto">trauma, </span><span data-contrast="auto">substance use disorders,</span><span data-contrast="auto"> mood disorders,</span><span data-contrast="auto"> and</span><span data-contrast="auto"> other</span><span data-contrast="auto"> mental health concerns. </span><span data-contrast="auto">When you choose to participate in one of Timberline Knolls’ programs</span><span data-contrast="auto">, you </span><span data-contrast="auto">will</span><span data-contrast="auto"> benefit from </span><span data-contrast="auto">our </span><span data-contrast="auto">support within a safe and inclusive environment. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<h2 aria-level="2"><span data-contrast="none">Residential Treatment</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":40,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></h2>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Each woman or girl </span><span data-contrast="auto">who </span><span data-contrast="auto">receives care in</span><span data-contrast="auto"> our residential program </span><span data-contrast="auto">follows a</span><span data-contrast="auto">n individualized</span><span data-contrast="auto"> </span><span data-contrast="auto">treatment plan</span><span data-contrast="auto">.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Depending on </span><span data-contrast="auto">your unique strengths, needs, and goals</span><span data-contrast="auto">, your </span><span data-contrast="auto">programming</span><span data-contrast="auto"> </span><span data-contrast="auto">may include the following elements:</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<ul>
<li><i><span data-contrast="auto">Detoxification</span></i><span data-contrast="auto">: Ambulatory, nonmedical detox within the residential environment</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><i><span data-contrast="auto">Medication management services</span></i><span data-contrast="auto">: Medications administered by members of our nursing staff</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><i><span data-contrast="auto">Basic medical care</span></i><span data-contrast="auto">: Noninvasive services to promote stability </span><span data-contrast="auto">and</span><span data-contrast="auto"> support, provided by </span><span data-contrast="auto">a </span><span data-contrast="auto">medical doctor or nurse practitioner</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><i><span data-contrast="auto">Individual therapy</span></i><span data-contrast="auto">: Typically two sessions each week with a licensed therapist</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><i><span data-contrast="auto">Group therapy</span></i><span data-contrast="auto">: Multiple daily groups, including </span><span data-contrast="auto">psychoeducational and process groups, all led by experienced professionals</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><i><span data-contrast="auto">Family therapy</span></i><span data-contrast="auto">: Usually one session per week, led by a licensed therapist</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><i><span data-contrast="auto">Experiential therapies</span></i><span data-contrast="auto">: Including recreational therapy, yoga, </span><span data-contrast="auto">dance/movement</span><span data-contrast="auto"> therapy, </span><span data-contrast="auto">art therapy</span><span data-contrast="auto">,</span><span data-contrast="auto"> </span><span data-contrast="auto">and supervised outings</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><i><span data-contrast="auto">Meal support</span></i><span data-contrast="auto">:</span><span data-contrast="auto"> May include dietary consultation </span><span data-contrast="auto">and </span><span data-contrast="auto">support during all meals and snack times</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
</ul>
<h2 aria-level="2"><span data-contrast="none">Age-Appropriate </span><span data-contrast="none">Programming</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":40,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></h2>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">To ensure </span><span data-contrast="auto">that </span><span data-contrast="auto">each person receives the focused, age-appropriate </span><span data-contrast="auto">programming</span><span data-contrast="auto"> that best addresses their needs</span><span data-contrast="auto">, our residential services are provided </span><span data-contrast="auto">in </span><span data-contrast="auto">six </span><span data-contrast="auto">lodges.</span><span data-contrast="auto"> </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">The lodge format provides a safe and supportive environment where women and girls can receive care alongside others within the same age group who have been experiencing similar challenges. </span><span data-contrast="auto"><a href="/about/location/">Our lodges</a> also promote the development of positive</span><span data-contrast="auto"> peer groups</span><span data-contrast="auto">,</span><span data-contrast="auto"> </span><span data-contrast="auto">which help our residents learn how to establish</span><span data-contrast="auto"> healthy </span><span data-contrast="auto">interpersonal relationships</span><span data-contrast="auto"> and </span><span data-contrast="auto">begin</span><span data-contrast="auto"> to trust others for </span><span data-contrast="auto">friendship and </span><span data-contrast="auto">support</span><span data-contrast="auto">.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">T</span><span data-contrast="auto">he t</span><span data-contrast="auto">ypical length of stay in</span><span data-contrast="auto"> our</span><span data-contrast="auto"> residential </span><span data-contrast="auto">program</span><span data-contrast="auto"> for adult women is about 33 days</span><span data-contrast="auto">, while the t</span><span data-contrast="auto">ypical length of stay for adolescent girls is about 40 days. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<h2 aria-level="2"><span data-contrast="none">Experienced Teams</span><span data-contrast="none"> of Professionals</span><span data-contrast="none"> </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":40,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></h2>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Care</span><span data-contrast="auto"> </span><span data-contrast="auto">in our residential program</span><span data-contrast="auto"> </span><span data-contrast="auto">is</span><span data-contrast="auto"> provided by a multidisciplinary <a href="/admissions/">team of experts</a>. Depending on your </span><span data-contrast="auto">specific needs</span><span data-contrast="auto">, you may work with the following professionals: </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<ul>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">Licensed social workers (LSWs)</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">Licensed clinical social workers (LCSWs)</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">Master’s-level social workers (MSWs)</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">Licensed professional counselors (LPCs)</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">Licensed clinical professional counselors </span><span data-contrast="auto">(</span><span data-contrast="auto">LCPCs)</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">Certified alcohol and drug counselors (CADCs)</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">Registered dieti</span><span data-contrast="auto">t</span><span data-contrast="auto">ians (RDs)</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">L</span><span data-contrast="auto">icensed dieti</span><span data-contrast="auto">t</span><span data-contrast="auto">ian nutritionist</span><span data-contrast="auto">s</span><span data-contrast="auto"> (LDNs)</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">Registered nurses (RNs)</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">Licensed practical nurses (LPNs)</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">Behavioral health associates (BHAs)</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">Medical doctor</span><span data-contrast="auto">s</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">Psychiatrist</span><span data-contrast="auto">s</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">Christian therapist</span><span data-contrast="auto">s</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">Art therapist</span><span data-contrast="auto">s</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">Dance/movement therapist</span><span data-contrast="auto">s</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">Yoga therapist</span><span data-contrast="auto">s</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
</ul>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Throughout your time </span><span data-contrast="auto">in our residential program</span><span data-contrast="auto">, you’ll be encouraged to work in active collaboration with the members of your treatment team and empowered to advocate on your own behalf </span><span data-contrast="auto">so </span><span data-contrast="auto">that </span><span data-contrast="auto">you can</span><span data-contrast="auto"> take ownership of your recovery.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<h2 aria-level="2"><span data-contrast="none">Partial Hospitalization Program </span><span data-contrast="none">(PHP)</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":40,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></h2>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">In addition to residential </span><span data-contrast="auto">programming</span><span data-contrast="auto">, we also offer a partial hospitalization program (PHP) for adult women</span><span data-contrast="auto"> that includes on-campus housing options.</span><span data-contrast="auto"> </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">You may</span><span data-contrast="auto"> step down to our PHP after completing </span><span data-contrast="auto">the </span><span data-contrast="auto">residential </span><span data-contrast="auto">program</span><span data-contrast="auto">, or you may enter treatment directly at the PHP level. </span><span data-contrast="auto">In </span><span data-contrast="auto">PHP, you</span><span data-contrast="auto"> have</span><span data-contrast="auto"> the option of taking part in </span><span data-contrast="auto">programming </span><span data-contrast="auto">five days per week or seven days per week. </span><span data-contrast="auto">The specific program details as they apply to each woman</span><span data-contrast="auto">’s</span><span data-contrast="auto"> </span><span data-contrast="auto">care are </span><span data-contrast="auto">made on an individual basis</span><span data-contrast="auto"> according to what’s best for </span><span data-contrast="auto">her.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">PHP </span><span data-contrast="auto">consists of</span><span data-contrast="auto"> daylong programming that</span><span data-contrast="auto"> can</span><span data-contrast="auto"> include the following elements of care:</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<ul>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">Basic medical care: Available as needed</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559685":360,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">Medication management: Including weekly meeting with psychiatrist</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559685":360,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">Group therapy: Multiple sessions during each day</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559685":360,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">Individual therapy: Two sessions per week</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559685":360,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">Family therapy: Usually one session per week</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559685":360,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">Experiential therapy: Varies depending on need</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559685":360,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
</ul>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">T</span><span data-contrast="auto">he t</span><span data-contrast="auto">ypical length of stay in </span><span data-contrast="auto">our </span><span data-contrast="auto">partial hospitalization program</span><span data-contrast="auto"> is two to three weeks.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<h2 aria-level="2"><span data-contrast="none">TK Academy</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":40,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></h2>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">To ensure that adolescent girls </span><span data-contrast="auto">can make appropriate academic progress</span><span data-contrast="auto"> during their time in our residential program, </span><span data-contrast="auto">our on-site TK Academy provides educational support services.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">TK Academy is staffed by certified teachers who are specialists in English, math, science, and social studies. Our teachers have experience working with students of all learning levels from middle school through high school</span><span data-contrast="auto">. These compassionate professionals are</span><span data-contrast="auto"> prepared to help young people who have been experiencing concerns such as school anxiety, school refusal, disorganization, and perfectionism.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Students remain enrolled in their home school districts while they’re in our residential program. The TK Academy staff </span><span data-contrast="auto">collaborates with representatives from these districts, </span><span data-contrast="auto">students’ </span><span data-contrast="auto">parents </span><span data-contrast="auto">or</span><span data-contrast="auto"> </span><span data-contrast="auto">guardians</span><span data-contrast="auto">,</span><span data-contrast="auto"> and our therapeutic </span><span data-contrast="auto">teams </span><span data-contrast="auto">to ensure that each girl receives superior, personalized educational service</span><span data-contrast="auto">s</span><span data-contrast="auto"> in a manner that reflects and supports </span><span data-contrast="auto">her</span><span data-contrast="auto"> continued </span><span data-contrast="auto">recovery</span><span data-contrast="auto">. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<h2 aria-level="2"><span data-contrast="none">Alumn</span><span data-contrast="none">ae </span><span data-contrast="none">Services</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":40,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></h2>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Each woman</span><span data-contrast="auto"> or girl</span><span data-contrast="auto"> who completes </span><span data-contrast="auto">programming </span><span data-contrast="auto">at Timberline Knolls receives a detailed discharge plan to guide her continued progress. She also becomes eligible to participate in our vibrant alumn</span><span data-contrast="auto">ae</span><span data-contrast="auto"> program. Alumn</span><span data-contrast="auto">ae</span><span data-contrast="auto"> services include:</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<ul>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">One year of free aftercare support</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">Annual alumnae retreat</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">Monthly alumnae night</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">Private Facebook page</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li><span data-contrast="auto">Supportive connections with our nationwide network of TK alumnae</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
</ul>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Prior to your discharge, our alumnae coordinator will introduce you to the many benefits that are available to you through this program.</span></p>

Treatment Program for Females | Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment 
 
 

 

 

 
 

 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 
 Skip to Content 
 

 

 
 
 
 

 

 

 
 
 Menu 
 

 
 (630) 749-2493 
 
 

 

 
 
 Menu 
 

 
 
 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 
 
 Successful Treatment Results 

 Testimonials 
 
 

 

 

 
 
 
 

To request medical records: CALL (615) 861-6000 x 5 or

 Email Your Request 

 
 
 
 

 
 Treatment Programs for Women & Girls 

 Timberline Knolls offers  individualized and  age-appropriate services for girls and women whose lives have been impacted by eating disorders ,  trauma,  substance use disorders,  mood disorders,  and  other  mental health concerns.  When you choose to participate in one of Timberline Knolls’ programs , you  will  benefit from  our  support within a safe and inclusive environment.    

 Residential Treatment   

 Each woman or girl  who  receives care in  our residential program  follows a n individualized   treatment plan .   

 Depending on  your unique strengths, needs, and goals , your  programming   may include the following elements:   

 

 Detoxification : Ambulatory, nonmedical detox within the residential environment   

 Medication management services : Medications administered by members of our nursing staff   

 Basic medical care : Noninvasive services to promote stability  and  support, provided by  a  medical doctor or nurse practitioner   

 Individual therapy : Typically two sessions each week with a licensed therapist   

 Group therapy : Multiple daily groups, including  psychoeducational and process groups, all led by experienced professionals   

 Family therapy : Usually one session per week, led by a licensed therapist   

 Experiential therapies : Including recreational therapy, yoga,  dance/movement  therapy,  art therapy ,   and supervised outings   

 Meal support :  May include dietary consultation  and  support during all meals and snack times   

 

 Age-Appropriate  Programming   

 To ensure  that  each person receives the focused, age-appropriate  programming  that best addresses their needs , our residential services are provided  in  six  lodges.     

 The lodge format provides a safe and supportive environment where women and girls can receive care alongside others within the same age group who have been experiencing similar challenges.  Our lodges also promote the development of positive  peer groups ,   which help our residents learn how to establish  healthy  interpersonal relationships  and  begin  to trust others for  friendship and  support .   

 T he t ypical length of stay in  our  residential  program for adult women is about 33 days , while the t ypical length of stay for adolescent girls is about 40 days.    

 Experienced Teams  of Professionals     

 Care   in our residential program   is  provided by a multidisciplinary team of experts . Depending on your  specific needs , you may work with the following professionals:    

 

 Licensed social workers (LSWs)   

 Licensed clinical social workers (LCSWs)   

 Master’s-level social workers (MSWs)   

 Licensed professional counselors (LPCs)   

 Licensed clinical professional counselors  ( LCPCs)   

 Certified alcohol and drug counselors (CADCs)   

 Registered dieti t ians (RDs)   

 L icensed dieti t ian nutritionist s  (LDNs)   

 Registered nurses (RNs)   

 Licensed practical nurses (LPNs)   

 Behavioral health associates (BHAs)   

 Medical doctor s   

 Psychiatrist s   

 Christian therapist s   

 Art therapist s   

 Dance/movement therapist s   

 Yoga therapist s   

 

 Throughout your time  in our residential program , you’ll be encouraged to work in active collaboration with the members of your treatment team and empowered to advocate on your own behalf  so  that  you can  take ownership of your recovery.   

 Partial Hospitalization Program  (PHP)   

 In addition to residential  programming , we also offer a partial hospitalization program (PHP) for adult women  that includes on-campus housing options.     

 You may  step down to our PHP after completing  the  residential  program , or you may enter treatment directly at the PHP level.  In  PHP, you  have  the option of taking part in  programming  five days per week or seven days per week.  The specific program details as they apply to each woman ’s   care are  made on an individual basis  according to what’s best for  her.   

 PHP  consists of  daylong programming that  can  include the following elements of care:   

 

 Basic medical care: Available as needed   

 Medication management: Including weekly meeting with psychiatrist   

 Group therapy: Multiple sessions during each day   

 Individual therapy: Two sessions per week   

 Family therapy: Usually one session per week   

 Experiential therapy: Varies depending on need   

 

 T he t ypical length of stay in  our  partial hospitalization program  is two to three weeks.   

 TK Academy   

 To ensure that adolescent girls  can make appropriate academic progress  during their time in our residential program,  our on-site TK Academy provides educational support services.   

 TK Academy is staffed by certified teachers who are specialists in English, math, science, and social studies. Our teachers have experience working with students of all learning levels from middle school through high school . These compassionate professionals are  prepared to help young people who have been experiencing concerns such as school anxiety, school refusal, disorganization, and perfectionism.   

 Students remain enrolled in their home school districts while they’re in our residential program. The TK Academy staff  collaborates with representatives from these districts,  students’  parents  or   guardians ,  and our therapeutic  teams  to ensure that each girl receives superior, personalized educational service s  in a manner that reflects and supports  her  continued  recovery .    

 Alumn ae  Services   

 Each woman  or girl  who completes  programming  at Timberline Knolls receives a detailed discharge plan to guide her continued progress. She also becomes eligible to participate in our vibrant alumn ae  program. Alumn ae  services include:   

 

 One year of free aftercare support   

 Annual alumnae retreat   

 Monthly alumnae night   

 Private Facebook page   

 Supportive connections with our nationwide network of TK alumnae   

 

 Prior to your discharge, our alumnae coordinator will introduce you to the many benefits that are available to you through this program. 

 Our Programs 

 About Us 

 

 
 We help each woman and adolescent girl rediscover and harness her strengths in five core aspects of the self: 

 
 
 

 

 

 “ Going to Timberline Knolls was one of the best decisions I have ever made! I highly recommend you go here for treatment! You will come out knowing how to cope better with life! ” 

– Tiffany L.

 

 
 Why Choose Timberline Knolls? 

 

To view this embedded video, please edit your cookie preferences and allow Personalization cookies.

 Cookie Preferences 

 Individualized Care from Exemplary Medical and Clinical Staff 

 Experiential & Spiritual Reinvigoration and Healing for the Whole Family 

 Lifelong Friendships with a Positive Peer Group 

 Secure, Tranquil Setting in a Convenient, Central Location 

 Award Winning On-Campus School 

 
 

 

Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment Center is respected nationwide for its treatment of eating disorders, mental health, and addiction concerns.

 

 

 © 2026 Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment Center / 40 Timberline Drive, Lemont, IL 60439 / (630) 749-2493 

 

If you are unable to read or view this page please call Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment Center at (630) 749-2493 .

 

 

 
 Accessibility Notice 

 Privacy Practices 

 Online Privacy Policy 

 Compliance & Code of Conduct 

 Cookie Preferences 
 

 

 
 

 
 Call 24/7 
 Menu

 Cookie Preferences 
 

 
 
 

 
 

 
 
 Top 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Timberline Knolls is Closed

Timberline Knolls is honored to have served the community for nearly 20 years. We have now closed our campuses and are no longer accepting new patients. For additional resources, please visit  https://www.samhsa.gov/ .

        <script>
            /////////////////////////////////////////////
            /// [S T A R T]  O S A N O
            /////////////////////////////////////////////
            let initialCount = 0;
            let iFramesLoaded = false;
            let chatLoaded = false;
            // Count how many of the three consents are true
            function countConsents() {
                let c = Osano.cm;
                let count = 0;
                if (c.analytics) count++;
                if (c.marketing) count++;
                if (c.personalization) count++;
                return count;
            }

            //Wait for document to render or chat/iframes fire too early on implied consent
            document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function () {
                // Run once Osano finishes initializing
                Osano.cm.addEventListener('osano-cm-initialized', function() {
                    initialCount = countConsents();
                    if (Osano.cm.personalization) {
                        loadIframeEmbeds();
                        loadNiceChat();
                    }
                });

                Osano.cm.addEventListener('osano-cm-consent-saved', function() {
                    const newCount = countConsents();
                    if (Osano.cm.personalization) {
                        loadIframeEmbeds();
                        loadNiceChat();
                    }
                    // If any consent is removed, refresh page
                    if (newCount < initialCount) {
                        location.reload();
                    } else {initialCount = newCount;}
                });

                const showCookiePreferences = document.getElementsByClassName('show-cookie-preferences');
                const link = document.getElementById('osano_drawer');
                if (!link) return;
                // Ensure CMP is ready
                Osano.cm.addEventListener('osano-cm-initialized', function () {
                    link.addEventListener('click', function (e) {
                        e.preventDefault();
                        if (Osano.cm.drawerOpen) {
                            Osano.cm.hideDrawer();
                        } else {
                            // Most installs: no args; some docs show a token—fallback if needed.
                            try { Osano.cm.showDrawer(); }
                            catch { Osano.cm.showDrawer('osano-cm-dom-info-dialog-open'); }
                        }
                    });
                });
                // Hook to drawer
                for (i=0;i<showCookiePreferences.length;i++) {
                    showCookiePreferences.item(i).addEventListener('click', function () {
                        Osano.cm.showDrawer();
                    });
                }
            });
            /////////////////////////////////////////////
            /// [E N D]  O S A N O
            /////////////////////////////////////////////
            function loadNiceChat() {
                            }
            function loadIframeEmbeds() {
                if (!iFramesLoaded) {
                    const embeddedIframes = document.querySelectorAll('.acadia_iframe_inner, .acadia_video_inner');
                    for (i=0;i<embeddedIframes.length;i++) {
                        const embeddedIframe = embeddedIframes.item(i);
                        embeddedIframe.innerHTML = '';
                        const iframe = document.createElement('iframe');
                        iframe.setAttribute('src', embeddedIframe.getAttribute('data-src'));
                        iframe.setAttribute('class', embeddedIframe.getAttribute('data-class'));
                        iframe.setAttribute('data-cookie-consent', 'yes');
                        iframe.setAttribute('allowfullscreen', '');
                        iframe.setAttribute('loading', 'lazy');
                        embeddedIframe.appendChild(iframe);
                    }
                    iFramesLoaded = true;
                }
            }
        </script>

class="acadia_headline acadia_headline_1 hero-h1 sp"><span class="headline"><span class="text">Residential Treatment Program for Women & Girls</span></span></h1></div></div></div></div><div class="vc_row-full-width vc_clearfix sp"></div></div></div></div><div id="main" class="sp30"><div class="container clearfix vc_row"><div id="content" class="vc_column vc_col-xs-12 vc_col-md-8 sp30"><div id="article"><article id="post-1390" class="article acadia_page_cats-admissions"><div class="entry-content"><div class="wpb-content-wrapper"><div class="vc_row vc_row_15 vc_row-fluid sp"><div class="vc_column vc_column_16 vc_column_container vc_col-sm-12 sp"><div class="vc_column-inner "><div class="wpb_wrapper"><div class="acadia_text acadia_text_1 sp"><p><span data-contrast="auto">Timberline Knolls</span><span data-contrast="auto"> </span><span data-contrast="auto">provides age-appropriate residential </span><span data-contrast="auto">programming</span><span data-contrast="auto"> for</span><span data-contrast="auto"> adolescent</span><span data-contrast="auto"> girls and </span><span data-contrast="auto">adult </span><span data-contrast="auto">women. </span><span data-contrast="auto">Our residential program is a safe and inclusive environment where all are welcomed and supported.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<h2 aria-level="2"><span data-contrast="none">Benefits of Residential </span><span data-contrast="none">Programming</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":40,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></h2>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Residential programming at Timberline Knolls offers many benefits for girls and women</span><span data-contrast="auto">, including</span><span data-contrast="auto">:</span><span data-contrast="auto"> </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<ul>
<li data-leveltext="" data-font="Symbol" data-listid="13" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="0" data-aria-level="1"><span data-contrast="auto">Individualized </span><span data-contrast="auto">services</span><span data-contrast="auto">: When you enter our residential program, you will complete a thorough evaluation and receive a unique plan to guide your care. Your team will monitor your progress and update your plan as necessary throughout your time with us.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li data-leveltext="" data-font="Symbol" data-listid="13" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="0" data-aria-level="1"><span data-contrast="auto">Round-the-clock care: Members of your team will be available 24/7 to provide support and guidance </span><span data-contrast="auto">whenever you need help.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li data-leveltext="" data-font="Symbol" data-listid="13" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="0" data-aria-level="1"><span data-contrast="auto">S</span><span data-contrast="auto">afe and supportive residential environment: When you take part in our residential program, you will live in one of six lodges. This promotes a sense of community and allows you to share support with others </span><span data-contrast="auto">whose experiences and goals are similar to yours</span><span data-contrast="auto">.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134233279":true,"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
</ul>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">O</span><span data-contrast="auto">nce you complete our residential program, you </span><span data-contrast="auto">will have a</span><span data-contrast="auto"> </span><span data-contrast="auto">lifetime membership in our alumnae program, which </span><span data-contrast="auto">can be a </span><span data-contrast="auto">vibrant </span><span data-contrast="auto">source of </span><span data-contrast="auto">ongoing</span><span data-contrast="auto"> support</span><span data-contrast="auto">.  </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<h2 aria-level="2"><span data-contrast="none">Elements of Care</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":40,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></h2>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Our focus on <a href="/programs/">individualized service</a> means that no two girls or women will </span><span data-contrast="auto">have </span><span data-contrast="auto">identical experiences at Timberline Knolls. Your care in our residential program will be guided by a unique plan that reflects and addresses your specific strengths, needs, and goals. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Depending on those factors, your plan may include</span><span data-contrast="auto"> ambulatory</span><span data-contrast="auto">,</span><span data-contrast="auto"> nonmedical detoxification</span><span data-contrast="auto">;</span><span data-contrast="auto"> medication management</span><span data-contrast="auto">;</span><span data-contrast="auto"> basic medical services</span><span data-contrast="auto">;</span><span data-contrast="auto"> and individual, group, family, and experiential </span><span data-contrast="auto">therap</span><span data-contrast="auto">ies</span><span data-contrast="auto">. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">A typical day in our residential program includes multiple group sessions, including experiential, psychoeducational, and process groups. Topics that may be addressed include </span><span data-contrast="auto">coping skills, dialectical behavior </span><span data-contrast="auto">therap</span><span data-contrast="auto">y (DBT) skills,</span><span data-contrast="auto"> and</span><span data-contrast="auto"> food discovery. Experiential groups may include recreatio</span><span data-contrast="auto">nal activities</span><span data-contrast="auto">, yoga, dance and movement, and supervised outings.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">During an average week, most girls and women take part in individual</span><span data-contrast="auto"> therapy</span><span data-contrast="auto"> sessions and family session. The frequency of these types of </span><span data-contrast="auto">sessions</span><span data-contrast="auto"> may be adjusted based on what’s best for you.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">If you have been struggling with your relationship with food, you may also participate in dietary consultations and receive monitoring and support services during meals and snacks. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<h2 aria-level="2"><span data-contrast="none">Your Team</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":40,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></h2>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">While you are in our</span><span data-contrast="auto"> residential </span><span data-contrast="auto">program, </span><span data-contrast="auto">you will work with a multidisciplinary team of experienced professionals. These compassionate experts are committed to providing</span><span data-contrast="auto"> </span><span data-contrast="auto">individualized service</span><span data-contrast="auto">s</span><span data-contrast="auto"> in a supportive and respectful manner.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Depending on your unique needs, your team may include medical doctors, psychiatrists, nurses, social workers, counselors, </span><span data-contrast="auto">therap</span><span data-contrast="auto">ists, dieti</span><span data-contrast="auto">t</span><span data-contrast="auto">ians, nutritionists, </span><span data-contrast="auto">behavioral health associates, </span><span data-contrast="auto">and other professionals as needed. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Taking part in residential programming at Timberline Knolls is not a passive experience. Your team will encourage you to share your thoughts and insights, </span><span data-contrast="auto">provide honest feedback, </span><span data-contrast="auto">ask questions, and</span><span data-contrast="auto"> express any concerns you’re having. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Your time in our residential program will be relatively brief. But the care you receive, the skills you develop, and the lifestyle changes you make will have lifelong benefits. Your team will empower you to play an active role in the process so </span><span data-contrast="auto">that </span><span data-contrast="auto">you will be prepared to make continued progress after you’ve transitioned out of residential programming. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><i><span data-contrast="auto">This content was reviewed and approved by the clinical staff at Timberline Knolls.</span></i><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>

sp" data-testimonial-cat-id="" data-testimonial-id="877"><div class="acadia_testimonial"><div class="notimage"><div class="quote"><p><span class="openquote">“</span>Going to Timberline Knolls was one Residential Program for Females | Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment 
 
 

 

 

 
 

 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 
 Skip to Content 
 

 

 
 
 
 

 

 

 
 
 Menu 
 

 
 (630) 749-2493 
 
 

 

 
 
 Menu 
 

 
 
 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 
 
 Successful Treatment Results 

 Testimonials 
 
 

 

 

 
 
 
 

To request medical records: CALL (615) 861-6000 x 5 or

 Email Your Request 

 
 
 
 

 
 Residential Treatment Program for Women & Girls 

 Timberline Knolls   provides age-appropriate residential  programming  for  adolescent  girls and  adult  women.  Our residential program is a safe and inclusive environment where all are welcomed and supported.   

 Benefits of Residential  Programming   

 Residential programming at Timberline Knolls offers many benefits for girls and women , including :     

 

 Individualized  services : When you enter our residential program, you will complete a thorough evaluation and receive a unique plan to guide your care. Your team will monitor your progress and update your plan as necessary throughout your time with us.   

 Round-the-clock care: Members of your team will be available 24/7 to provide support and guidance  whenever you need help.   

 S afe and supportive residential environment: When you take part in our residential program, you will live in one of six lodges. This promotes a sense of community and allows you to share support with others  whose experiences and goals are similar to yours .   

 

 O nce you complete our residential program, you  will have a   lifetime membership in our alumnae program, which  can be a  vibrant  source of  ongoing  support .     

 Elements of Care   

 Our focus on individualized service means that no two girls or women will  have  identical experiences at Timberline Knolls. Your care in our residential program will be guided by a unique plan that reflects and addresses your specific strengths, needs, and goals.    

 Depending on those factors, your plan may include  ambulatory ,  nonmedical detoxification ;  medication management ;  basic medical services ;  and individual, group, family, and experiential  therap ies .    

 A typical day in our residential program includes multiple group sessions, including experiential, psychoeducational, and process groups. Topics that may be addressed include  coping skills, dialectical behavior  therap y (DBT) skills,  and  food discovery. Experiential groups may include recreatio nal activities , yoga, dance and movement, and supervised outings.   

 During an average week, most girls and women take part in individual  therapy sessions and family session. The frequency of these types of sessions  may be adjusted based on what’s best for you.   

 If you have been struggling with your relationship with food, you may also participate in dietary consultations and receive monitoring and support services during meals and snacks.    

 Your Team   

 While you are in our  residential  program,  you will work with a multidisciplinary team of experienced professionals. These compassionate experts are committed to providing   individualized service s  in a supportive and respectful manner.   

 Depending on your unique needs, your team may include medical doctors, psychiatrists, nurses, social workers, counselors,  therap ists, dieti t ians, nutritionists,  behavioral health associates,  and other professionals as needed.    

 Taking part in residential programming at Timberline Knolls is not a passive experience. Your team will encourage you to share your thoughts and insights,  provide honest feedback,  ask questions, and  express any concerns you’re having.    

 Your time in our residential program will be relatively brief. But the care you receive, the skills you develop, and the lifestyle changes you make will have lifelong benefits. Your team will empower you to play an active role in the process so  that  you will be prepared to make continued progress after you’ve transitioned out of residential programming.    

 This content was reviewed and approved by the clinical staff at Timberline Knolls.   

 Our Programs 

 About Timberline Knolls 

 

 
 We help each woman and adolescent girl rediscover and harness her strengths in five core aspects of the self: 

 
 
 

 

 

 “ Going to Timberline Knolls was one of the best decisions I have ever made! I highly recommend you go here for treatment! You will come out knowing how to cope better with life! ” 

– Tiffany L.

 

 
 Why Choose Timberline Knolls? 

 

To view this embedded video, please edit your cookie preferences and allow Personalization cookies.

 Cookie Preferences 

 Individualized Care from Exemplary Medical and Clinical Staff 

 Experiential & Spiritual Reinvigoration and Healing for the Whole Family 

 Lifelong Friendships with a Positive Peer Group 

 Secure, Tranquil Setting in a Convenient, Central Location 

 Award Winning On-Campus School 

 
 

 

Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment Center is respected nationwide for its treatment of eating disorders, mental health, and addiction concerns.

 

 

 © 2026 Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment Center / 40 Timberline Drive, Lemont, IL 60439 / (630) 749-2493 

 

If you are unable to read or view this page please call Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment Center at (630) 749-2493 .

 

 

 
 Accessibility Notice 

 Privacy Practices 

 Online Privacy Policy 

 Compliance & Code of Conduct 

 Cookie Preferences

 Menu 
 

 
 (630) 749-2493

Timberline Knolls is Closed

Timberline Knolls is honored to have served the community for nearly 20 years. We have now closed our campuses and are no longer accepting new patients. For additional resources, please visit  https://www.samhsa.gov/ .
