Residential & Outpatient Treatment Center for Women & Girls
Residential Program
What is residential treatment?
When you take part in residential care at Timberline Knolls, you can step away from the stresses and distractions of daily life so that you can focus your full attention on your health.
During residential treatment, you’ll live in a comfortable room on our serene , 43-acre campus.
Our residential programming features round-the-clock care and individualized services, all provided by compassionate professionals in a safe and welcoming environment.
What distinguishes Timberline Knolls from other women-only rehabs or residential treatment centers?
Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment Center is respected nationwide for its treatment of eating disorders, mental health, and addiction concerns.
© 2026 Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment Center / 40 Timberline Drive, Lemont, IL 60439 / (630) 749-2493
If you are unable to read or view this page please call Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment Center at (630) 749-2493 .
Admissions Information & What To Expect | Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment Center
In general, our residential programming may be ideal for you if you meet the following criteria:
If you are an adult age 18 or older who do es not need residential care, we also offer a partial hospitalization program (PHP) with on-campus housing options .

When you first get to our facility for residential treatment , you’ll have a brief session with one of our clinicians, you’ll sign some paperwork, and you’ll complete a medical assessment with a member of our nursing staff.

Timberline Knolls will also inform you of our full continuum of care available – from residential to PHP (partial hospitalization program) and IOP (Intensive Outpatient Program), with supportive housing in nearby Orland Park, IL.
Intensive Outpatient Program for Females | Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment
Girls ages 12-17 may enter our adolescent IOP directly, or they may step down to the program after completing a higher level of care, such as inpatient or residential programming.
The adolescent IOP is appropriate for girls who do not need the round-the-clock care offered in residential treatment but can still benefit from participating in a structured program.
Leading Experiential Therapy & Groups for Females | Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment
Drawing on the personal recovery experiences of several of our team members, the Timberline Knolls staff designed our residential treatment program with emphasis on empowering the spirit.
The Timberline Knolls Spirituality program helps each woman harness the hope she will call on during residential treatment and throughout a lifetime of recovery as she works to:
To learn more about how we help women renew their spirit during residential treatment, please call us.
At Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment Center, we utilize two types of art therapy: the directed approach and the open studio process.
The benefits of art therapy for a woman or girl in treatment at Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment Center are enormous.

Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment Center also provides individual DMT sessions for residents who need special attention to meet the needs related to the processing of trauma, body image and/or self-expression.
At Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment Center, we utilize three different yoga approaches, offered in three separate groups.
Yoga therapy is an appropriate therapeutic intervention for the many disorders and addictions we treat at Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment Center.
I was admitted to residential treatment at Timberline Knolls in 2016 to treat my depression.
I also started to unpack my relationship with my father, with my father and our family therapist; a process that I prepared for in residential.

Residential treatment at Timberline Knolls and continuing with partial hospitalization ensured that I could keep building on my recovery, namely in my relationships with spirituality, with new people, and with myself.
Partial Hospitalization Program for Females | Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment
The Importance of Education in Residential Treatment

When it comes to recovery from eating disorders, addiction, and other co-occurring disorders, we believe the combination of individualized and group therapy, a therapeutic residential treatment environment, and supportive education are critical.
Treatment Program for Females | Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment
Residential Treatment
Each woman or girl who receives care in our residential program follows a n individualized treatment plan .
Detoxification : Ambulatory, nonmedical detox within the residential environment
To ensure that each person receives the focused, age-appropriate programming that best addresses their needs , our residential services are provided in six lodges.
T he t ypical length of stay in our residential program for adult women is about 33 days , while the t ypical length of stay for adolescent girls is about 40 days.
Care in our residential program is provided by a multidisciplinary team of experts .

Throughout your time in our residential program , you’ll be encouraged to work in active collaboration with the members of your treatment team and empowered to advocate on your own behalf so that you can take ownership of your recovery.
In addition to residential programming , we also offer a partial hospitalization program (PHP) for adult women that includes on-campus housing options.
You may step down to our PHP after completing the residential program , or you may enter treatment directly at the PHP level.
To ensure that adolescent girls can make appropriate academic progress during their time in our residential program, our on-site TK Academy provides educational support services.
Students remain enrolled in their home school districts while they’re in our residential program.

rawMentions: Residential & Outpatient Treatment Center for Women & Girls
Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP)

Timberline Knolls will also inform you of our full continuum of care available – from residential to PHP (partial hospitalization program) and IOP (Intensive Outpatient Program), with supportive housing in nearby Orland Park, IL.
Our outpatient services are available as a step-down or direct admission.
Intensive Outpatient Program for Females | Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment
Intensive Outpatient Program for Women and Girls
That’s what Timberline Knolls’ intensive outpatient program (IOP) delivers.
Benefits of Intensive Outpatient Programming

An intensive outpatient program is an opportunity for you to continue receiving professional care for an eating disorder, a mental health concern, the effects of trauma, or a substance use disorder after completing a higher level of programming.

Flexibility: The IOP provides a higher level of support than you would receive in traditional outpatient therapy, but it offers more freedom to work, attend school, or participate in other activities than a PHP does .
Programming in our intensive outpatient program takes place three to five days a week, depending on your specific needs.
The adolescent intensive outpatient program is available for girls ages 12-17.

Our evening intensive outpatient program (IOP) is a great treatment option for women who are struggling with mood and trauma-related disorders and need more structure and support than outpatient therapy alone can provide.
After all, that mantra is what led you to our intensive outpatient program.

The supported housing environment advances our clients’ recovery journeys and compliments our Continuing Care Outpatient Program by offering individuals the structure and support of recovery- focused living.

rawMentions: Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP)
If you are an adult age 18 or older who do es not need residential care, we also offer a partial hospitalization program (PHP) with on-campus housing options .

Timberline Knolls will also inform you of our full continuum of care available – from residential to PHP (partial hospitalization program) and IOP (Intensive Outpatient Program), with supportive housing in nearby Orland Park, IL.
Women can directly admit to our PHP.
At Timberline Knolls, women who complete our partial hospitalization program (PHP) can step down to our IOP as they continue to address the concerns that have affected their lives.
For adults, admission to the IOP at Timberline Knolls currently requires the prior completion of our partial hospitalization program.

Flexibility: The IOP provides a higher level of support than you would receive in traditional outpatient therapy, but it offers more freedom to work, attend school, or participate in other activities than a PHP does .

Camaraderie: Much like with our PHP , you’ll participate in group therapy sessions that will allow you to build bonds with women who have struggled with eating disorders and other behavioral health concerns.
If you have already completed PHP at our facility, our staff of expert clinicians will be able to adjust your previous plan to reflect the progress you made at that higher level of care.

Partial Hospitalization Program for Females | Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment
Partial Hospitalization Program for Women
In many cases, t he partial hospitalization program (PHP) environment can be an ideal space for women to successfully progress in their recovery.
To help meet you where you are in your recovery journey, Timberline Knolls offers a partial hospitalization program for women age 18 and older .
Benefits of Partial Hospitalization Programming
The partial hospitalization program at Timberline Knolls can help you address the concerns that have disrupted your daily life.
Our expert staff can help you determine if our partial hospitalization program is the best fit for you or if you woul d benefit from a different level of care.
That’s why w e individualize care for every woman who takes part in the partial hospitalization program at Timberline Knolls.
With those influences in mind, you’ll take part in our partial hospitalization program five or seven days a week for up to three weeks depending on your specific needs and progress.
But the days, weeks, and months after you complete the partial hospitalization program at Timberline Knolls are just as important as your time in our care.
Our PHP empowers women to live the lives they deserve.
There are countless benefits to choosing the PHP at Timberline Knolls , including:
Structured e nvironment: When you take part in our PHP, you will follow a structured schedule each day that ensures that you receive a high level of care.
Real-life skill – building : You’ll have the opportunity to practice the skills and coping techniques you learn in our PHP in real-life environments and bring any learning back to the program.
Depending on your current needs, y ou may step down to our PHP from a higher level of care, or you may enter directly at the PHP level.
At the Timberline Knolls PHP, we offer supportive living housing options to our clients at an affordable cost.
The PHP Experience
We start creating your discharge plan within 48 hours after you begin taking part in our PHP so that we can provide you with the resources you need to continue your progress.
In addition to residential programming , we also offer a partial hospitalization program (PHP) for adult women that includes on-campus housing options.
T he t ypical length of stay in our partial hospitalization program is two to three weeks.
You may step down to our PHP after completing the residential program , or you may enter treatment directly at the PHP level.
In PHP, you have the option of taking part in programming five days per week or seven days per week.

rawMentions: Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP)

Timberline Knolls will also inform you of our full continuum of care available – from residential to PHP (partial hospitalization program) and IOP (Intensive Outpatient Program), with supportive housing in nearby Orland Park, IL.
Intensive Outpatient Program for Females | Timberline Knolls Residential Treatment
Intensive Outpatient Program for Women and Girls
That’s what Timberline Knolls’ intensive outpatient program (IOP) delivers.
Benefits of Intensive Outpatient Programming

An intensive outpatient program is an opportunity for you to continue receiving professional care for an eating disorder, a mental health concern, the effects of trauma, or a substance use disorder after completing a higher level of programming.
Programming in our intensive outpatient program takes place three to five days a week, depending on your specific needs.
The adolescent intensive outpatient program is available for girls ages 12-17.

Our evening intensive outpatient program (IOP) is a great treatment option for women who are struggling with mood and trauma-related disorders and need more structure and support than outpatient therapy alone can provide.
After all, that mantra is what led you to our intensive outpatient program.
We also provide a separate, age-specific adolescent IOP for girls ages 12-17.
At Timberline Knolls, women who complete our partial hospitalization program (PHP) can step down to our IOP as they continue to address the concerns that have affected their lives.
Girls ages 12-17 may enter our adolescent IOP directly, or they may step down to the program after completing a higher level of care, such as inpatient or residential programming.
There are many benefits to the support you’ll receive by choosing the IOP at Timberline Knolls, including:

Flexibility: The IOP provides a higher level of support than you would receive in traditional outpatient therapy, but it offers more freedom to work, attend school, or participate in other activities than a PHP does .
Skill-building practice: With the additional freedom of flexibility that comes with our IOP, you’ll have the opportunity to apply the skills you’ve learned to situations in your everyday life.
For adults, admission to the IOP at Timberline Knolls currently requires the prior completion of our partial hospitalization program.
The Adult IOP Experience
As is the case with any program at Timberline Knolls, our IOP takes into account each woman’s unique needs, life experience, medical history, and genetic background.
Typical length of stay is flexible, but most women take part in our IOP for five to six weeks.
The Adolescent IOP Experience
The adolescent IOP is appropriate for girls who do not need the round-the-clock care offered in residential treatment but can still benefit from participating in a structured program.
In our IOP, we provide specialized services for girls who are suffering from eating disorders and other mental health concerns, including:
Group therapy is the main treatment method we use in our adolescent IOP.
The Evening IOP Experience
Our evening IOP is in-person and available on Mondays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays from 5:30PM-8:30PM.
That’s why we start creating a unique discharge plan for you as soon as you begin taking part in our IOP.

By beginning this valuable resource right away, we can provide you with the support you need to continue your progress, and we’ll update that document to reflect the progress you make during your time in our IOP.

rawMentions: Mood Disorder, Drug Addiction, Co-Occurring Eating Disorders, Alcohol Treatment Center | Residential Rehab & Recovery | Timberline Knolls
FOR WOMEN & ADOLESCENT GIRLS WHO ARE STRUGGLING WITH ADDICTION, DEPRESSION, TRAUMA, & CO-OCCURRING EATING DISORDERS
Timberline Knolls provides individualized care for adult women and adolescent girls who have been struggling with substance use disorders, mental health concerns, and co-occurring eating disorders.
A characteristic of eating disorders, addictions and co-occurring disorders, is the tendency to detach from feelings by focusing on obsessive thoughts, concrete thinking and/or body distortions.

When it comes to recovery from eating disorders, addiction, and other co-occurring disorders, we believe the combination of individualized and group therapy, a therapeutic residential treatment environment, and supportive education are critical.
